_comment: Authoritative recipe to reproduce demo/LoanWhiz-demo.mp4 without transcript archaeology. Two steps: (1) prodemo create_demo with these exact params (the app must be running on :3000 serving web/public/ — cards + mcp-terminal.html — and the prodemo MCP must be connected); (2) ffmpeg bookend with FRESHLY DERIVED cut points (see post_processing). Narration count MUST equal 1 + total_uiMoves + 1.

uiMoves.value: What is the Class A distribution this period, and what is it grounded in?

narration: In response to the challenge, we have built deterministic primitives that do the maths — the agent only orchestrates, never the arithmetic — and a compiler that turns every prospectus into a real, working model.

That's a library of nine governed primitives — from the prospectus extractor that compiles the deal, through the waterfall runner and covenant monitor, to the audit logger. Each one returns its output with a confidence score, citations, and an audit entry.
And they're reusable: another team's agent connects to our governed MCP server and reads the catalogue — all nine primitives.
It calls the ESMA tape normaliser, then the waterfall runner — the same to-the-cent engine — and gets typed results back.
Every call carries the governed envelope — output, confidence, citations, and audit — without rewriting a single primitive.
Is it correct? We validate the engine against a real, seasoned deal.
We start with a deal where we have only partial data —
Green Lion 2024: the prospectus and the published report, but no loan tapes.
Even so, it reproduces the deal's published waterfall to the cent — eleven of eleven revenue steps, four of four redemption — and every result stays governed. The framework is robust to partial data.
And where we do have the loan tapes, like our primary deal, we get the fuller picture: live pool analytics straight off the loan-level data.
It's all driven by a live, governed agent — ask it anything, in plain English.
What is the Class A distribution this period, and what is it grounded in?
It plans the primitive calls, then computes the answer.
Eleven point four million to Class A — grounded in the exact waterfall steps.
And every answer carries a full governance evidence pack —
confidence, citations, and data provenance, on every result.

post_processing._comment: The rendered body carries throwaway intro ('LoanWhiz.') + outro ('That's LoanWhiz.') beats and prodemo's own cards. We re-bookend with clean 1s SILENT intro+outro cards and trim those two throwaway beats. CUT POINTS ARE RENDER-SPECIFIC — derive them per render, do NOT reuse: run `ffmpeg -i RAW -af silencedetect=noise=-30dB:d=0.30 -f null -`; SS = ~0.4s BEFORE the framing speech starts (i.e. land inside the silent gap after the dropped intro beat, NOT exactly on the speech — a tight cut makes the open feel abrupt; aim for a ~0.4-0.5s lead-in breath so the framing card is on screen before it talks); TO = a point inside the silence gap right before the final ~0.9s 'That's LoanWhiz.' beat (i.e. just after the last real-content beat ends).
post_processing.ffmpeg: FF=$(/home/uzair/prodemo/.venv/bin/python -c 'import imageio_ffmpeg;print(imageio_ffmpeg.get_ffmpeg_exe())'); "$FF" -y -loop 1 -t 1 -i web/public/card-intro.png -ss <SS> -to <TO> -i RAW.mp4 -loop 1 -t 1 -i web/public/card-outro.png -f lavfi -t 1 -i anullsrc=r=44100:cl=mono -f lavfi -t 1 -i anullsrc=r=44100:cl=mono -filter_complex "[0:v]scale=1920:1080,fps=30,format=yuv420p[iv];[1:v]scale=1920:1080,fps=30,format=yuv420p[bv];[2:v]scale=1920:1080,fps=30,format=yuv420p[ov];[iv][3:a][bv][1:a][ov][4:a]concat=n=3:v=1:a=1[v][a]" -map "[v]" -map "[a]" -c:v libx264 -preset medium -crf 20 -pix_fmt yuv420p -c:a aac -ar 44100 -ac 1 demo/LoanWhiz-demo.mp4
post_processing.verify: Confirm: duration ~2:04; first 1s and last 1s mean_volume ~ -91 dB (silent bookends); a terminal frame (~step=3) shows 'data_source direct'; a terminal frame (~step=4) shows 'reserve_fund_shortfall_trigger OK · 100% funded'.

last_render.note: ss gives a ~0.46s lead-in breath before the framing line (first narration at 1.456s) so the open isn't abrupt
